logos.name: Jumper X — White
logos.description: White X symbol on transparent background
usageGuidelines: Use on dark backgrounds for maximum contrast
Minimum size: 24px × 24px
Maintain clear space of 8px around the logo
Do not alter the color or apply effects

logos.name: Jumper X — Black
logos.description: Black X symbol on transparent background
usageGuidelines: Use on light backgrounds for maximum contrast
Minimum size: 24px × 24px
Maintain clear space of 8px around the logo
Do not alter the color or apply effects

logos.name: Jumper Studio — Full Logo White
usageGuidelines: Use on dark backgrounds for brand presence
Minimum width: 120px
Maintain clear space equal to the height of the 'J'
Do not stretch or condense horizontally

logos.name: Jumper Studio — Full Logo Black
usageGuidelines: Use on light backgrounds for brand presence
Minimum width: 120px
Maintain clear space equal to the height of the 'J'
Do not stretch or condense horizontally

gradients.name: Organic Gradient 01
gradients.description: First organic gradient — warm tones with orange and purple blend
usageGuidelines: Use for hero sections and large backgrounds
Overlay with semi-transparent black (rgba(0,0,0,0.4)) for text readability
Works best with white text

gradients.name: Organic Gradient 02
gradients.description: Second organic gradient — purple and orange swirl
usageGuidelines: Use for card backgrounds or section dividers
Pair with white or light text
Can be used at reduced opacity (0.3-0.5) behind content

gradients.name: Organic Gradient 03
gradients.description: Third organic gradient — vibrant orange to purple
usageGuidelines: Use for high-impact sections (hero, call-to-action)
Requires text overlay with good contrast
Best with white or very light text

gradients.name: Organic Gradient 04
gradients.description: Fourth organic gradient — deep purple with orange highlights
usageGuidelines: Use for dark-themed sections
Excellent contrast with white text
Works well as a subtle background (opacity 0.2-0.4)

gradients.name: Organic Gradient 05
gradients.description: Fifth organic gradient — soft orange and purple blend
usageGuidelines: Use for medium-impact backgrounds
Pair with white or light gray text
Can be used full-opacity or reduced

gradients.name: Organic Gradient 06
gradients.description: Sixth organic gradient — purple dominance with orange accents
usageGuidelines: Use for premium or feature sections
Best with white text for contrast
Effective at 50-70% opacity over dark surfaces

gradients.name: Organic Gradient 07
gradients.description: Seventh organic gradient — warm orange gradient with subtle purple
usageGuidelines: Use for energetic, vibrant sections
Excellent for CTAs and hero backgrounds
White text strongly recommended

samples.text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog

typography.fallbackStack: Haffer, Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif
installInstructions: 1. Download the Haffer font files (OTF or TTF)
2. For web: Add @font-face declarations in your CSS
3. For desktop: Install font files in your system's font directory
4. Use the fallback stack for reliable cross-platform rendering
usageGuidelines: Use weight 600 (semibold) for headings
Use weight 400 (regular) for body text
Use weight 700 (bold) sparingly for emphasis
Apply fluid clamp sizes for responsive typography
Maintain 1.6 line-height for body text, 1.2 for headings

usageExamples.description: Use the white X logo on dark backgrounds (black, dark gray, or organic gradients) for maximum contrast and brand presence.
Don't use the white logo on light backgrounds — it becomes invisible and loses impact.
Maintain clear space around logos (minimum 8px). This ensures the logo breathes and isn't crowded by other elements.
Don't stretch, skew, or distort logos. Maintain the original aspect ratio at all times.
Use organic gradients as full-width backgrounds with white text overlays for hero sections and call-to-action areas.
Don't use gradients at full opacity behind dense text — it creates readability issues. Use semi-transparent overlays instead.
